title: CFTR / ivacaftor
summary: Patients with the CC genotype (do not have a copy of the CFTR S977F variant) and cystic fibrosis have an unknown response to ivacaftor treatment, as response may depend on the presence of other CFTR variants. Other genetic and clinical factors may also influence response to ivacaftor.
riskSummary: CFTR variant associated with efficacy for ivacaftor
detail: Patients with the CC genotype (do not have a copy of the CFTR S977F variant) and cystic fibrosis have an unknown response to ivacaftor treatment, as response may depend on the presence of other CFTR variants. Other genetic and clinical factors may also influence response to ivacaftor.
whyItMatters: PharmGKB curates pharmacogenomic evidence from the literature, with level 1A–2B annotations backed by expert review and published clinical studies.
notes: PharmGKB annotations reflect published literature and should not replace clinical pharmacogenomic testing.

title: CFTR / ivacaftor
summary: Patients with the CT genotype (one copy of the CFTR S977F variant) and cystic fibrosis may respond to ivacaftor treatment. FDA-approved drug labeling information and CPIC guidelines indicate use of ivacaftor in cystic fibrosis patients with at least one copy of S977F. Other genetic and clinical factors may also influence response to ivacaftor.

title: Lower body mass index
summary: Lower body mass index
detail: The rs33977706 polymorphism (-820G > T) was associated with a lower body mass index (BMI) (p = 0.004).
